items.title: Spurs announce signing of Wemby to a multi-year contract extension.
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt:
Spurs announce signing of Wemby to a multi-year contract extension. 

Terms not disclosed by team, but a league source confirmed @ShamsCharania report that it's a five-year, $252 million maximum rookie-scale extension, with a player option in the fifth season.

items.title: Egor Demin was solid and Nets got a strong first half from 2-way Chaney Johnson.
items.author: John Hollinger
items.body_excerpt:
Egor Demin was solid and Nets got a strong first half from 2-way Chaney Johnson.

OTOH ... Danny Wolf parlayed his ~50 dribbles into zero points and 1 assist, Drake Powell was invisible, Mikel Brown struggled, and Ben Saraf was outplayed by an actual hobbit.

items.title: Victor Wembanyama locks himself into the 25% max (contract on the left), saving the Spurs $10 million annually should he qualify for the 30% max.
items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
Victor Wembanyama locks himself into the 25% max (contract on the left), saving the Spurs $10 million annually should he qualify for the 30% max.

He would’ve qualified if he wins MVP, Defensive Player of the Year, or makes All-NBA next season. That possibility is now eliminated with no escalators.

Sounds like Bam treated Tyler like Amen Thompson did.

items.author: NBA Stat
items.body_excerpt:
Bam Adebayo and Tyler Herro got into an altercation in Las Vegas on Friday in which Bam struck Tyler.

Herro made disparaging comments toward Adebayo last week from his alternate Instagram account:

“You should get paid 60 million to be a top tier defender on some nights? I’m just wondering”

#NBA

items.title: Pacers beat the Cavs 99-93. Game got dicey after they built a 20-point lead, but the Pacers held on. Rienk Mast with a double-double, Yuki Kawamura with a very Yuki game, and Jalen Slawson put up numbers. 1-0 start for the summer Pacers.
items.author: Tony East
items.body_excerpt: Pacers beat the Cavs 99-93. Game got dicey after they built a 20-point lead, but the Pacers held on. Rienk Mast with a double-double, Yuki Kawamura with a very Yuki game, and Jalen Slawson put up numbers. 1-0 start for the summer Pacers.

items.title: Summer Pacers take Game 1 99-93 They had a dominant 3Q, then the Cavs threatened in the 4th.
items.author: Scott Agness
items.body_excerpt:
Summer Pacers take Game 1 99-93 They had a dominant 3Q, then the Cavs threatened in the 4th.

A 16/11 double-double from center Rienk Mast. Slawson had 15-6-5. Kawamura impressed with his passing and ability to get others open. Peter (13) 3 was the dagger.

Next: Sat. v 76ers
